_notes: This file is embedded in the axon CLI at build time and used as
the single source of truth for servo-model metadata, parameter
defaults, and bundled firmware file references.
AUDIENCE SEPARATION:
Each parameter entry has two layers. User-facing fields
(vendor_label, description, unit, modes, min, max, values,
docs_url) are what the end user sees via 'axon get <param>
--help', tooltips in a GUI, or 'axon read' output. Keep these
plain-language and action-oriented. All the maintainer / agent
detail (byte offset, encoding, widget offset, source file
references, confidence levels, TBD formulas, etc.) lives inside
the nested 'implementation' object and is NEVER shown to end
users. The CLI's help renderer MUST NOT read from the
implementation object.
TARGET BOOTLOADER: V1.3 (iProduct = 'USBBootloader V1.3').
The MK2 programmer (bootloader V1.4) adds parameters that are NOT
in the V1.3 set — specifically Left/Right Limit (distinct from
Servo Angle), Refresh Rate, and Power-On Sound. Those MK2-only
parameters are intentionally OMITTED from this catalog; they are
out of scope for v1.0 and tracked as a post-v1.0 epic.
SERVO MODES: Axon V1.3 servos operate in one of two firmware
modes (Servo Mode or CR Mode). Different parameters are valid
in each mode — 'axon set <param>' must reject attempts to set
a parameter that does not apply in the current mode. See the
top-level 'servo_modes' object and the per-parameter 'modes'
field.
The canonical V1.3 parameter list is sourced from the vendor's
archive docs and enumerates exactly nine parameters: Servo
Angle, Servo Neutral, Dampening Factor, Sensitivity, PWM Power,
Soft Start, Inversion, Loose PWM Protection, and Overload
Protection. This catalog implements the ones we currently have
a confident byte mapping for; the rest (Dampening Factor, Soft
Start, Overload Protection) are listed under '_not_yet_mapped'
and error out cleanly in the CLI.
Model IDs (the `id` key in 'models') are the ASCII bytes at
config offset 0x40..0x47 with trailing '*' as padding. Only the
Axon Mini has been confirmed empirically so far (SA33****); Max
and Micro are placeholders pending a physical read from those
models.
IMPORTANT TERMINOLOGY: 'Sensitivity' (vendor name) is the PWM
dead-band. 'Dampening Factor' (vendor name) is the D coefficient
in the internal position-control PID loop. They are two
DIFFERENT things and live at different config-block offsets.

servo_mode.name: Servo Mode
servo_mode.description: The default mode. Your PWM pulse width commands a target angle and the servo holds that position. Use this for arms, grippers, aimers, and anything else where you want the servo to go to a specific place and stay there.

implementation.decomp_ui_panel: FUN_00405518 (primary overlay — ~25 writes, the full parameter set)
implementation.notes: Detected at runtime from the identify reply: rx[5] == 0x03. Axon's identify reply format is 'FF FF 01 06 00 <MODE_ID> 02 01 08 <chksum>' on the wire; over HID the equivalent bytes appear at rx[5].

cr_mode.name: CR Mode (Continuous Rotation)
cr_mode.description: The servo is re-purposed as a variable-speed gearmotor: PWM pulse width commands a rotation speed (and direction) instead of a target angle. Use this when you want continuous spinning — flywheels, conveyor wheels, drum rollers.

implementation.decomp_ui_panel: FUN_00406248 (secondary overlay — only 3 byte writes: 0x12, bits in 0x25, and 0x36)
implementation.notes: Detected at runtime from the identify reply: rx[5] == 0x04. In this mode most position-control parameters (angle, deadband, PID damping, overload protection) are ignored by the firmware.

unavailable_parameters.servo_angle: Continuous rotation has no fixed range
unavailable_parameters.sensitivity: Dead-band around neutral is only meaningful when holding a position
unavailable_parameters.dampening_factor: The PID D coefficient controls position tracking, not speed
unavailable_parameters.overload_protection: Vendor docs: '3-stage in Servo Mode; unavailable in CR Mode'

implementation.notes: First 4 bytes of the 95-byte config block identify which servo variant the block is for. A Mini's block starts with '3b d0 0b f6'.
BE u16 at 0x0A..0x0B = 0x0050, mirrored at 0x27..0x28, 0x29..0x2A, 0x2B..0x2C. Vendor docs say raw 0-255 maps to 0-355 deg, so raw 80 ≈ 111°. Unverified; needs A/B .svo diff.
byte 0x06 = 0x80; encoding 'stored = user_us + 0x80', so raw 128 → 0 µs offset (centered at 1500 µs).
byte 0x0C = 0x50; encoding 'stored = (user_step + 1) * 16'. Step 0 = 'ultra high' (1 µs deadband); step 4 is a mid-range deadband.

Bits 0x60 of byte 0x25 set to 0x60 on the live Mini. Three modes exist (Release / Hold / Neutral); which numeric bit value maps to which mode is still unknown — needs a three-way .svo A/B diff to confirm.
byte 0x11 = 0xDC mirrored at 0x12, 0x13, and 0x0F (= primary − 0x14). 220/255 ≈ 86%, which matches vendor's recommended default of 85% @ 6.0v.

_Axon_Max_placeholder._TODO: Fill in once we can read a Max servo. Model ID unknown.
_Axon_Max_placeholder.name: Axon Max

implementation.notes: Unknown until we get a physical Max to read.

Physically read from an Axon Micro in CR mode on 2026-04-10. Model id at offset 0x40..0x47 is the ASCII 'SA20BHS*' (7 chars + 1 padding '*'). Manufactured 2023-10-05 per the vendor exe screenshot. The full 95-byte default CR-mode config block is checked in as research/saleae-captures/micro-cr-initial-connection.csv and mirrored as a live read via the axon CLI. The Micro bundled firmware files use space-separated names from the vendor download page (different from Mini/Max underscore convention).

defaults._note: Defaults captured from a factory-fresh Axon Micro in CR mode. Servo-mode defaults are TBD (requires flashing the Micro to Servo Mode with 'Axon Micro Servo Mode.sfw' and re-reading).

implementation.notes: byte 0x06 = 0x80 → 0 us offset from the 1500us neutral.
byte 0x11 = 0xDC mirrored at 0x12, 0x13. 220/255 ≈ 86% — matches the vendor exe's displayed PWM Power: 86.3% for this servo.
byte 0x36 = 0xFF → 25.5s, matching the vendor exe's displayed ProPTL: 25.5 s.

servo_angle.description: How far the servo sweeps from end to end. Servo Mode only — in CR Mode the servo rotates continuously and this setting has no effect.

implementation.deg_formula_note: Per vendor docs but not verified by A/B .svo diff yet.
implementation.notes: The widget writes the BE u16 to bytes 0x0A..0x0B and replicates it to three mirror slots. Each mirror is an internal rate-limit register initialized from the user's Servo Angle setting.

servo_neutral.description: Adjusts the PWM pulse width at which the servo is centered (Servo Mode) or stopped (CR Mode). Measured in microseconds, offset from the default 1500 µs. Keep adjustments small — large changes can cause unexpected behavior.

implementation.notes: Widget label confirmed via VCL event-handler symbol 'rztrNeutralChange' at 0x007a9888. Vendor docs specify the unit is microseconds.

sensitivity.description: The size of the 'dead zone' around the centered position where the servo ignores small PWM changes. Higher sensitivity = smaller dead zone = more responsive to tiny commands (but can jitter on noisy signals). Servo Mode only.

implementation.notes: Vendor docs specify 'Ultra high = 1us' so step 0 (raw 0x10) gives a 1 µs dead-band. Mapping from higher steps to microseconds is unknown; would be filled in by a .svo A/B diff with different Sensitivity settings.

inversion.description: Reverses the servo's direction. Default is CCW (normal). Use 'reversed' if you mounted the servo upside-down or backwards relative to how you want it to move.

implementation.confidence_note: Byte/bit location derived from the checkbox-toggle→persistent-bit pattern in FUN_00405518 lines 88-108. No literal 'Inversion' label string in data_printable_runs.txt; a .svo A/B diff would promote this to high confidence.
implementation.notes: A secondary 'dirty' bit at mask 0x01 is toggled each time the user changes the checkbox, but only bit 0x02 is the persistent setting.

loose_pwm_protection.description: What the servo does when it loses the PWM signal but still has power. 'release' goes limp (no torque). 'hold' holds the last commanded position. 'neutral' returns to the center / zero-speed position.

implementation.value_mapping_note: Byte/bit location is certain from FUN_00405518 lines 179-192. Which specific bit value (0x00, 0x40, or 0x60) corresponds to which vendor mode name is unknown. CLI should expose 'get' as a raw-bit annotation until an A/B .svo diff resolves the mapping, and should reject 'set' attempts with a clear message.
implementation.notes: Two bits of the flags byte at 0x25 encode a 3-way enum. Mapping bit values to vendor mode names needs a three-way .svo A/B diff.

proptl.description: An overheat-prevention timeout, in seconds, that automatically cuts the servo's output after continuous operation for this long. Only applies to the Axon Micro in CR mode. The maximum is 25.5 seconds (the factory default); there is no 'disabled' setting. If your application needs continuous rotation for longer, you will need to stop and restart the servo. The vendor exe calls this 'ProPTL' (undocumented in the public Axon docs).

implementation.source: decomp_slider_overlay_0x357_00406248.c line 29 + axon_servo_programming_exe screenshot + Axon Micro CR mode .svo capture
implementation.notes: Only the CR-mode overlay function FUN_00406248 writes this byte (line 29), confirming it is not used in servo mode. Cross-referenced against a physical Axon Micro (model id SA20BHS*) where the observed raw value 0xFF corresponds exactly to the vendor exe's displayed value of 25.5 s — confirming the raw*0.1 scale. Per prior user research, this is a Micro-specific feature; other models may not have it in their CR-mode firmware.

pwm_power.description: Caps the maximum power the servo will draw, as a percentage. Lower values help on current-limited power supplies. Recommended defaults: 75% at 4.8 V, 85% at 6.0 V (for the Mini). Applies in both modes.

implementation.percent_formula_note: The live raw 0xDC (220) maps via this formula to ~86%, matching the vendor's recommended 85% @ 6.0V Mini default — a good cross-check but not an A/B confirmation.
implementation.notes: The 4-byte mirror pattern (0x11, 0x12, 0x13, 0x0F = primary - 20) is likely the main cap plus three progressive reductions for the Overload Protection state machine. In CR Mode the FUN_00406248 overlay writes the same value to byte 0x12 (one of the mirrors). Precise per-stage mapping requires the load-side decomp.

_not_yet_mapped._note: These are canonical V1.3 parameters (per the archive docs) that we cannot yet confidently map to specific bytes of the 95-byte config block. 'axon get <name>' and 'axon set <name>' will return 'not yet mapped' errors for these names and point users at docs/BYTE_MAPPING.md. They are still round-trippable via 'axon read --svo' / 'axon write --from cfg.svo'.

dampening_factor.description: How aggressively the servo resists overshoot when moving to a target angle. NOT RECOMMENDED to change — it's tuned at the factory. Servo Mode only.

implementation.reason_blocked: Label string 'Damping Factor: ' exists at 0x007a734a and VCL symbol 'rztrDampingChange' at 0x007a98b4 confirm the UI widget exists, but we haven't found the widget's offset or the byte position in the current decomp. Likely lives in the 0x35..0x3A 'advanced mode' range or in FUN_00406248. Needs FUN_00404b28 (the load-side function) decompiled to confirm.

soft_start.description: Limits how fast the servo can accelerate when it first powers on. Prevents sudden jerks. Both modes.

implementation.reason_blocked: Not referenced in any currently-decompiled function. Probably one of the unmapped widgets (p1+0x3dc, p1+0x3e0, p1+0x3e4 in FUN_00406248). Needs more decomp.

overload_protection.description: Automatically reduces the servo's maximum power when it's been stalling for too long, to protect the motor from burnout. Three progressive stages. Servo Mode only (unavailable in CR Mode).

implementation.reason_blocked: Partially implicated in the PWM Power mirror pattern, but we can't distinguish 'main PWM Power' from 'stage-N overload reduction' without the load-side decomp. Bytes 0x14..0x18 (five monotonically-decreasing values 41, 33, 29, 25, 20 on the live Mini) and 0x19..0x1E (three BE-u16 values of 0x003C = 60) are strong candidates for 'per-stage power curve' and 'per-stage duration' respectively.

_mk2_only_parameters_omitted._note: The following parameters exist in the Axon MK2 programmer (bootloader V1.4) but NOT in the V1.3 target we're building against. They are intentionally omitted from this catalog. Adding MK2 support requires a separate reverse-engineering pass on the MK2 exe and access to a V1.4-era dongle and servo; tracked as a post-v1.0 epic.
_mk2_only_parameters_omitted.left_limit: Distinct from Servo Angle in MK2; one-sided motion bound.
_mk2_only_parameters_omitted.right_limit: Distinct from Servo Angle in MK2; other-sided motion bound.
